context: The psalmist said this in Psalm 33 in verse number 14. Notice what the Bible says, excuse me, verse number 4 in Psalm 33 and verse number 4. For the word of the Lord is right and all his works are done in truth.

context: The law of the Lord is perfect, converting the soul. The testimony of the Lord is sure, making wise the simple.

context: Moreover by them is thy servant warned: and in keeping of them there is great reward.

context: thou shalt meditate therein day and night

context: Forever, O Lord, thy word is settled in heaven

context: if I regard iniquity in my heart, the Lord will not hear me

context: Do you remember what the psalmist said in Psalm 119?

point_summary: Psalm 139:13–18 is read as describing not the original creation of Adam and Eve but the wonder of subsequent human reproduction, extending God's direct creative work to every person conceived.

context: according to verse 13 and verse number 15, where? In the mother's womb.

context: according to verse 13 and verse number 15, where? In the mother's womb.
point_summary: God's intricate craftsmanship in forming the human body — described as "curiously wrought," language drawn from skilled embroidery — takes place in the mother's womb, establishing conception as the origin of God-made life.

context: He says in verse number 14, notice, he says marvelous are thy works.
point_summary: Human life's origin in the womb, confirmed by the Hebrew word for embryo appearing uniquely in this passage, grounds the declaration that life begins at conception and that God is its maker.

context: as we look at this passage in Psalm 139, a familiar passage of Scripture,
point_summary: The declaration "I am fearfully and wonderfully made" is grounded in God's valuing love, demonstrated ultimately in Christ dying for sinners who were his enemies.

context: And the record of Psalm 139 gives us the wonder of subsequent reproduction.
point_summary: Psalm 139's account of God knowing the internal organs, skeletal structure, and embryo from conception grounds the conviction that human life bears inherent value because it is made in God's image.

context: ...but Thou, O Lord, art a shield for me, my glory and the lifter up of mine head.

context: Psalm 28 and v. 7. The Lord is my strength and my shield. My heart trusted in Him and I am helped. Therefore, my heart greatly rejoiceth and with my song will I praise Him.

context: Psalm 33 and v. 20. Our soul waiteth for the Lord. He is our help and our shield.

context: the psalmist says to us there in Psalm 23, the Lord is my shepherd

context: Delight thyself also in the Lord, and He shall give thee the desires of thine heart.
point_summary: Psalm 37:4's promise that God gives the desires of the heart is frequently misread in isolation; the full verse conditions that gift on first delighting oneself in the Lord.

context: Commit thy way unto the Lord. Trust also in Him, and He shall bring it to pass.
point_summary: Committing one's way to the Lord and trusting Him is presented as a key indicator for aligning human will with God's will, in answer to the prayer "Thy will be done."

context: And He shall bring forth thy righteousness as the light, and thy judgment as the noonday.
point_summary: Psalm 37:6 is cited as one of four practical "nuggets" from Psalm 37:3–7 showing how believers can align their will with God's will in prayer.

context: Rest in the Lord, and wait patiently for Him. Fret not thyself because of Him, who prospereth in His way.
point_summary: Psalm 37:3–7 is read as a cluster of four practical indicators—trust, delight, commit, and rest—by which believers progressively align their own will with God's will in prayer.

context: His way is perfect.

context: You read Psalm 136, and you see every single verse in that chapter says the mercy of the Lord, it endureth forever, it endureth forever.

context: More to be desired are they than gold, yea, than much fine gold, sweeter also than honey and the honeycomb.

context: enter into His gates with thanksgiving

context: In Psalm 100 and verse number two, the Bible tells us to serve the Lord with gladness

context: In Psalm 84 in verse number nine, the Bible says, behold, oh God, our shield and look upon the face of thine anointed

context: For a day in thy court is better than a thousand. I had rather be a doorkeeper in the house of my God than to dwell in the tents of wickedness

context: For the Lord God is a son and shield. The Lord will give grace and glory. No good thing will he withhold from them that walk uprightly
point_summary: God withholds no good thing from those who walk uprightly, grounding the call to serve the Lord in His proven generosity toward His people.

context: Oh, Lord of hosts, blessed is the man that trusteth in thee
